transcript_quality: partial — Granola meeting summary available; call recording on Monday (file_mm1daxvv) but full transcript not accessible
lead_profile_text: ABROAD — Owner moving to Spain for 1-year trial from ~end July 2026. 4-bed house, 74 Torworth Road, Borehamwood, WD6 4ES. Furnished (owner retaining furniture for return); bar area with tap, large garden. Children's bedrooms require repaint to neutral; bunk beds require replacement with double + single setup. Partner's company requires periodic UK access — calendar blocking essential. Partner has not yet attended a meeting; follow-up call 21 May with partner present.
emotional_profile: Motivated by hard departure timeline (end of July), not abstract interest. No financial objections raised — figures accepted without challenge. Partner's sign-off is the remaining gate. Calendar-blocking and property protection details are the key operational requirements.
delay_trigger_phrases: moving out approximately end of July 2026
delay_timeline: Departing end of July 2026; August 2026 launch targeted
green_light_trigger_phrases: moving out approximately end of July 2026
1 year trial — she is planning to return

top_objections.text: Most bookings come within 30 days — can partner's periodic UK access needs be accommodated?
top_objections.zac_response: Date blocking is possible; existing bookings cannot be cancelled but dates must be planned in advance

top_objections.text: Partner's company requires UK access flexibility
top_objections.zac_response: Calendar blocking confirmed as possible; Stayful to confirm in writing before 21 May follow-up call

top_questions.text: Can partner's company periodic UK visits be accommodated within the STL calendar?
top_questions.answer: Yes — date blocking is possible with caveat that existing bookings cannot be cancelled; advance planning required since most bookings come in within 30 days of arrival

top_questions.text: Can partner's maintenance team be used for property maintenance?
top_questions.answer: Yes — subject to meeting Stayful's requirements (weekend/out-of-hours availability, specific response times); payments up to £300/month deductible from monthly statements

what_moved_them: Calendar access for date-blocking — directly resolved the partner's UK access requirement
Income figures (£86,961 gross / £45,222 net vs £16,800 LTL) — compelling uplift not challenged
Property protection measures: ID checks, £200 security deposit, £100,000 insurance, 1–3 annual inspections
30–40% direct bookings from trusted repeat guests
what_fell_flat: 30-day booking window caveat raised concern — resolved but should not be re-surfaced in follow-up

conversion_gaps.gap: Partner has not attended a meeting and has not yet agreed — 21 May partner call is his first exposure
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Send pre-call summary email to Noemi before 21 May that she can forward to her partner; ensure he arrives informed rather than cold; confirm calendar-blocking in writing before the call

offer_framing: 13%+VAT reduced from 15%; expires 31 May 2026. 6-month fixed term, 3-month notice period. No onboarding cost. Partner call 21 May before offer expiry.
zac_close_phrase: Follow-up call scheduled Thursday 21 May 2026 at 09:00 with partner present, ahead of 31 May offer expiry deadline
zac_income_intro: £86,961 gross / £45,222 net annually at £441/night and 54% occupancy; against long-let of £16,800/year — annual uplift +£30,102 (+199% vs LTL net)
zac_transition_phrases: Low competition market analysis — competitors averaging 24 reviews and 4.7 rating
zac_reassurance_phrases: £200 security deposit per guest, ID checks for all guests, £100,000 insurance, 1–3 annual inspections
30–40% direct bookings from trusted repeat guests
